article_title: EA Conducts Its Third Round of Layoffs This Year Across US and India as the $55 Billion Saudi-Led Buyout Nears Completion
summary: Clean follow-up on EA's third 2026 layoff round; every claim, financial specific, name, and date verified against cited snapshots, zero fabricated quotes. Sole finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning (no content correction warranted).

content_preview.title: EA Conducts Its Third Round of Layoffs This Year Across US and India as the $55 Billion Saudi-Led Buyout Nears Completion
content_preview.summary: EA cut an undisclosed number of jobs in customer support, IT, recruitment, and trust and safety across US remote roles and its Hyderabad office, its third layoff round of 2026 as the PIF-led buyout closes in.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Electronic Arts has carried out another round of job cuts, its third this year, affecting workers across the United States and India as the publisher moves toward completing the largest leveraged buyout in corporate history. According to [Insider Gaming](https://insider-gaming.com/ea-third-layoffs-55-billion-saudi-arabia-deal/), the cuts hit recruitment, customer support, trust and safety, and IT roles, while development positions were spared. The reductions land as EA is being acqu...

recommendations: Consider adding insider-gaming.com, gamerant.com, and tweaktown.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all are established gaming-industry outlets and recurring sources. Not edited as part of this review (allowlist warnings are non-blocking).

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-structured News piece (800 words). Clean Overview / What We Know / The Buyout Backdrop / What We Don't Know arc. Each paragraph attributes its claims inline to a named outlet. The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly flags the missing headcount, the unconfirmed closing date, and the unconfirmed CFIUS status — appropriate epistemic humility for a developing story.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 snapshots read from disk (gunzipped, status 200). source-0 Game Developer: confirms third round of 2026, Hyderabad India + US remote, departments (trust and safety, IT, customer support, recruitment), no number disclosed, prior Battlefield-division cuts (Criterion, DICE, Ripple Effect, Motive) and Full Circle/Skate (February), $55B buyout, 'EA declined to provide comment.' source-1 Insider Gaming: confirms third round, undisclosed number, non-development roles (recruitment/customer support/trust and safety/IT), 93.4% PIF stake, $55B Saudi deal. source-2 Game Rant: confirms US+India mix of remote and in-person roles, 'ten years or more' tenure, internal department-head message referencing changes to some roles / changes to what teams are covering / creating new roles, $55B PIF acquisition. source-3 Wikipedia: confirms $55B cost, $210/share, 25% premium, $36B equity, largest LBO to date, second-largest gaming acquisition after Microsoft/Activision Blizzard, PIF majority owner, Silver Lake significant minority, Affinity Partners five percent, CFIUS (Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States) approval needed, closure expected ~June 2026. source-4 TweakTown: confirms $55B buyout, December 22 shareholder vote, $210/share, 25% premium, Silver Lake + Jared Kushner's Affinity Partners, management remains intact with CEO Andrew Wilson continuing to lead.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every financial specific traces to a cited source: $55B (Wikipedia/TweakTown/Game Developer/Game Rant), $210/share and 25% premium (Wikipedia + TweakTown), $36B equity (Wikipedia), 93.4% PIF stake (Insider Gaming), December 22 vote (TweakTown), CFIUS / June 2026 closure (Wikipedia). Affected-studio list and the 'ten years or more' tenure detail are attributed to the outlets carrying them. The 'development positions were spared' phrasing is a faithful paraphrase of Insider Gaming's 'non-development roles' / 'does not seem to affect developer roles.' No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.no_fabricated_quotes: Programmatically scanned the body for quote-marked phrases: ZERO present. The contributor paraphrased every internal-message detail (changes to some roles, adjustments to what teams are covering, creating new roles) without quotation marks, so there is no verbatim-quote attribution to verify or break. This matches the contributor's stated approach.
editor_notes.framing_and_originality: Headline and lead correctly center on the NEW June 22 third-round layoffs; the buyout is treated as backdrop in a clearly subordinate section. The cross-reference to the May 27 article (EA's buyout shareholder vote + Canadian union national-security alarm) resolves to an existing article at src/content/articles/2026-05/27-eas-...md and uses the correct /article/YYYY-MM/slug URL format. This is a genuine follow-up, not a duplicate of the May 27 vote story.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual throughout. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference.
recommendations: insider-gaming.com, gamerant.com, and tweaktown.com are not yet on the source allowlist; recommend adding them (non-blocking, allowlist not edited as part of this review).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. The script's auto-verdict of APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS was driven solely by the allowlist warning, which is a non-blocking domain-registration matter, not a content error. There is no factual inaccuracy, misattribution, or fabricated quote that a corrections record could honestly document, so no corrections file is warranted. The article is clean and publishes as-is.
